mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets and rising wage pressures, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings improve.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong consumer demand and economic optimism, which drives S&P 500 forward returns through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and HOUST rise but 20d SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases, or their combined predictive power drops below 0.15 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes, which historically correlate with negative S&P 500 forward returns due to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals robust manufacturing activity, which drives S&P 500 forward returns through improved corporate earnings and economic confidence.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises >1.5% but 5d SPX returns are negative in > 30% of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with declining unemployment indicates a tightening labor market, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns through elevated corporate hiring and wage growth.
failure_condition: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls but 20d SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases, or the interaction effect's IC drops below 0.12.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending (measured by RSAFS) during a period of stable inflation indicates strong demand-side momentum, which drives S&P 500 forward returns through elevated corporate revenues.
failure_condition: RSAFS rises >2% but 5d SPX returns are negative in > 35% of cases, or the signal's IC falls below 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production amid rising inflation signals supply-side disruptions, which historically correlate with negative S&P 500 forward returns due to stagflationary pressures.
failure_condition: INDPRO falls and CPIAUCSL rises but 60d SPX returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect's IC drops below 0.10.
